msContents.summary: [p]Mrs Anne Brumwich her Booke of Receipts or Medicines ffor severall sores and other Infermities. [p]With many additions, by several later 17th cent. hands. [p]The original compiler, Anne Brumwich, writes in an early 17th cent. hand, but some of the additions are much later, one dated 1681 is found on p. 12. [p]Ccontains the following recipes, followed by an index: An excellent Soveraighne balsome called ye Lady Ropcues The vertues of this Balsome,A Precious balsome yt cureth sores old or new, ye goute paine in ye ioynts or bones the crampe, any manner of bruise itt is good against burnes & scalds Lucattilly his precious Balsome The vertues,A Soveraighne Balsome which will cure any greene wound presently An excellent Balsome curinge many disseases & all desperate wounds in ye space of 24 houres The virtues,Mris Anne Brumwich her Booke of Receipts or Medicines, For severall sores and other Infermities, A medicine for the pearle in the eye which Commoly commeth with a stroke in the eye and is a Little speck in the gray of the eye A Medecine For the staine in the eye, which is a Little dent or hole in the gray of the eye as big as a little pinns head, [5 signatures] Dorothy Cartwright,A water for an eye whose white is as red as flesh but the sight thereof is stile Cleere,A medicine for an eye that hath been swollen up toe or three dayes and the meanes not knowne how it came or for an eye that hath benne burned with fire A medicine For An eye that hath a mist or a thicke skinne over it by the use of toe many waters & yett some sight is Left in it A medicine for an eye that is dimme inclininge toe a Caterict A Remedy to take out a gritt stickinge in ye eye wch happeneth toe millers pickinge their mill A water to prsearve the sight of eye that is decayinge A water For Bleared eyes or that are sore in the corners of them Water for ye eyes The vertues of ye said water,A spedier way to make the said water An other way to make the like water much sooner A watter for eyes that are red & watterey aproved A medeson for any that hath a white spote growing in ther eyes A water that Cueres all sortes of sore eyes except catreyes A Resceipt for wattering eyes A receipt to stay the rueme in the eyes or teeth,A water of Exelent virtue to helpe ye weake or dimme sight of ye eyes when they are decayed by age Sicknes or other way A water to Stringthen the sight A powder for ye eyes To Cuer a blind horse or Cristane A water for sore eyes whose lides are all Swelled A Medicine for sore eyes For a pinne or pin & web in ye Eye A water for sore eyes commeded by Sr James Langham A very good receipt for sore eyes commended by Mis Worsley,A medicin for an ague quarterin or tertian Another Another for an ague To cuer any ague To cuer an ague A medicine for ye Ague Another For ye Same To take away ye heate Another Medicine for an ague for a child A Medicine for a third ague A Direcion to kill a ague Quotidia or tertian An aprooved drinke that cures all sorts of agues unlesse ye third day ague,A Medicine For a Quarterine ague A medicine for an Ague A drinke for an Ague wch hath cured some that hath had it 2 yeares together A present cure for an ague it hath often beene prooved and it cured An excellent easie Glister for a Child that hath any aguish distemper An easie purge for the same For all kinds of agues For all sorts of agues except the third day ague,A Medicine for ye quartan Ague For a Milcke brest yt hath taken cold and hardnesse gathered in it by reason of an ague For an Ague An aproved medison for all sorts of Agues: to be Layd on six houres before ye fit come: & if posible: used before they have had six fits A medeicne for an ague I had from my Lady falcland hath cuered third dayes agues Another aproved medicin for a quartern or any ague Medicines of Chirurgery, A salve for all manner of sores more especially for Fellons that doe breede in the ioynts of the fingers To make the pultise An other speciall salve For all sores New or ould Whatsoever,A salve to Cleanse an old sore of dead flesh A salve toe skinne an old sore A salve for an old soare or bloudy Canker A salve to heale a sore breast after yt it is broken with a pultise Another salve For soare Breasts or for burneings or scaldings To make the oyle of Eggs Toe make Mercury water which will kill a wild fire or heale an old soare A water to heale an old ulcer or fistula,A Cooleinge and healeinge plaister for ye same A drawinge plaister for ye same Another medicine for a fistula How to make a searecloth yt cures any greene wound or soare and Comforteth ye ioynts hurte by any blowe bruise fall or Lamenesse To make a bathe or poultise for a swollen legg full of humors issuinge out in little holes or ye skinne broken A Medicine to heale ye wild fire in a soare legg A Medicine For a wild fire or inflamation in ye face or els where A medicine or poultise toe heale a soare brest yt hath never soe many holes in it without tentinge A poultise to take an ague out of ye breast A medicine to keepe ye ague out of a breast yt is full of milke,A Medicine to heale a Fellon to take away ye swellinge without breakinge of it A Medicine to stay a bloudy fluckes or ye flowres if they come Imoderately For ye bloody flux A playster to heale a sore breast For a Fistula For to cuer & stop a felon Another for fellons & swellings For scurf & swellinge in ye breast,A medicine for ye Scurvie A present remedey for ye Scurvie An exellent powder for the scurvi A Medicine for ye Scurvy,The virtues The flower of all salves called Flowes Unguentorum good for all sores & swellings The Greene Ointment good for Aches Paines Bruses Swellings Scauldings, or Burnes good also to annoint the Stomacke for digestion and it must be maid in May A plaster to heall a burne Mathias his pretious oyntment of Balsimud,The vertus of flos unguatum,For the wind Coll'ck Another,To make a blake salve for an ach or wound For burnings or Scaldings Medicines phisicall For seaverall Diseases and Infirmities, A medicine to breake an Impostume in ye head ye which may be pteined by ye greate paine inwardly within ye eare and much Leanines in the head A medicine For ye stone A medicine For one yt through some fitt of ye stone or otherwise hath not made water in 8 or 10 dayes A Medicine For one yt Cannot make Water A Medicine to Dissolve the stone and soe Bringe it away by degrees,A excellent receipt For ye mother A medicine to give ease in ye payne of ye stone Dr Knowles plaister for ye back for ye wind Collick and stone A remedy for ye stone For ye ston to keep ye gravell from harden[ing] & to bring it away A Medicine For ye Collick yt cures when all other medicines fayle For ye wind Collik A Receipt for ye stone An aproved drink for ye stone in ye kidneys Another for ye same A Receipt to make ye stone watter it cueres ye partey that takes it A Soverain powder to cuer ye stone,A medecein for ye stone An aproved medeceine for ye stoane An Exelent drinke for ye Stone they that take it Shall never be trubled with noe great fites of it A Receipt for ye ston in ye Kidneys or bladr An excelent receipt for the stone comended by Mr Joines A Medicine For the cough of ye Longues The Lorde Aburganenines Receipt for ye Cough or consumption of the Longues,Another medicine For ye Consumption of ye longues Dia dragaguntum frigidum Cooling Loosinge for an hectick fever A very good medicene for a greate Cough A medeceine for ye Cough & for ye Consumption & tow make one fat allso it is very good for any thine body A Recipt for a Coufe of 20 years standing A Drink for one that hath a Cough & a Consumpton For a deepe Consumption with a feaver An Electuary for a Coufe of ye Longes or a Consumption Cough A powder to be given that cureth ye consumption of ye longes or vitalls & taketh away ye cough Anothere. barker,For a Cough Another for ye cough & to dry upe ye rume & fleame For a Cough A Receit for ye purginge of moist humors yt doth certainly cure & very much aprooved a Doctor offerd 100 for itt & could not obtaine itt A Medicine For ye Dropsie To make ye said diet Drinke To make ye said stomacher Another Medicine For ye Dropsie An aproved medecine for ye dropse,A Purge for ye Dropsie For ye Dropsie An Aprooved Medicine for a dry Dropsy or for one yt is swelled from head to foot wth wind For the Dropsie approoved A vomitation for ye Legge yt is full of ye Dropsy For ye dropsey To make a poultise toe Cure a soare breast though it be full of holes: without Tentinge A Soveraigne Pultise to breake a sore brest A Serecloth to disolve a sore brest An openinnge drinke for a breast yt is nott to bee broke,A drinke to dissolve an Ingendered hardnesse in a breast that looketh white & is not to bee broken A Powder to dissolve ye hardnesse in a breast yt is not to bee broke Another Powder for ye same A Poultise to breake a breast beinge very hard and bigge like a Cancer but white & loo#e from ye Ribbs For a Cancer in ye breast A Medicine toe Cure ye Jandise Another For ye same Another For ye Yellow Jandise For ye yellow Jandise when ye dissease hath taken ye mould of ye head For ye yellow Jandise,For ye blacke Jandise For ye yellow Jandise M Nixes Receit for a Consumption [untitled] To make snail watr For a cold or cough A Medicine to cure ye Canker in ye mouth An approoved Medicine For a Younge child yt hath a thrush in ye mouth An aproved powder for a canker in ye mouth when it eateth vollently An aproved medeceine for a Canker in ye mouth A vomitation to flesh & heale a bludey Canker or cancer in a legge For a canker in ye mouth. Lady Husseys receipt,A Medicine to Cure ye Fluxe or soare laske Another For ye same Another For ye same A Medicine For a Loosenesse Another For ye same Another for the same Another for ye same,A poultise to be applyed to ye navell to stay a loosnes of one yt is very weake or a woman in Child bed A glister to stay loosnesse A Medicine to cause one to goe to stoole yt hath not benne at stoole in a weeke or eight dayes A Medicine to keepe ye body Soluble An Electuary to help Costivenesse A Medicine to keepe ones body open A Caudle for ye bloody flicks For ye Flicks For a losenesse Another tested For a lask To stop a flux Another appoved by Mr Ayen To cuer a loosnesse Lady Husseys receipt,A Medicine For a sa#erlin or red face & pimpled with red spotts Another medicine For ye same Another For ye same Another For ye same Against ye inflemation of ye liver yt causeth pimpells,A Medicine to cure the Itch although it be never soe greate in any parte of ye bodie Another For ye same Mr Dingley For ye itch thoygh never soe bade A medecine for any teter or itch A present remedey for ye itch An easey purge for ye itch for a yong child A medicine to cure a worme To kill a ring worme or teter in Children or old folkes For a felon For ye Itch An excelent & sweet oyntmt for ye Itch To cuer ye Itch,A Medicine For such as cannot hold theire water A Medicine to strenthen ye Backe A Medicine For the wormes in a Child A medecine for wormes in young children To kill Maw wormes A Receipt for wormes A glister for a girle A glister for a boy,For ye pills To prevent Piles by ye small pox Another to prevent piling by ye small pox approved A Medicine For ye hemerods in the Fundament which will be rawe and itch and Ragged like wartes A Medicine For ye piles in ye Fundament being red soare akinge bleedinge especially when they goe to ye stoole & yt with great paine Another For ye same An oyntment For ye piles that are up in ye Reynes of ye Back and doe not appeare outwardly An oyntment for all kind swellings Burnings and scaldings Another for medecein for ye pils or hemrodes,An aproved Medeceine for ye pilles Another for ye same aproved Another for ye same For bleeding at the Nose Another Another by Dr. Willis order Another A Medecine to kill any Quick thinge as flye or flea that is crept into ye eare A Medicine to staunch Bleedinge att ye Nose or in a wound Another For ye same Another For the same Another For ye same Another For ye same Mr Dinglies Another for ye same A watter for bleeding with in or without Another to stanch bloud in ye nosterells A drinke for one that spits blude or some vaine broke in ye breast For bleeding at ye nose,A Medicine to pcure sleepe A Medceine to procure sleep & ease the paine in the head A Medicine For the sciatica or a greate paine in ye shoulder or hippe To draw out ye sciateco from ye huele bone A rare medicin for ye sicatica wn they have lost thir limbs for greng it curnd# a fisher man & was hed# him by a great phisitian because he was poor,Snaile watter against a Consumption A Lozeng made of snaills good for A Consumption A Tryed Medeceine for a Consumption or Coufe of the Longes A tryed receipt for a consumption & all inward ullcers it puevenyt# ye blud singularly as ueredly# it is a soverane medeceins for all evells of ye body A Medicine For ye runing of ye Reines Another for the same Another for the same Another for ye same A water for a Consumptive parson & to cleane the reines & strinthen the bake An exelent Restorative watter for one in weakenes it hase beene aproved by maney to be very good,A Medicine to prevent the scarres of ye small pockes in ye face A Medicine for a soare throate foe as one would thinke ye pallett of ye mouth to be downe foe yt they cannot swallow theire owne spittle or well speake Mr Rutkins excellent Receipt for any one yt hath a soare throate with ye small poxe it doth kill ye vename and poyson of ye small poxe both in ye throate and body A Medisone for the dispersing anny humour gathered to the Thorat or for any soarnes in the same A surope for the pallet of the month the amons of the eares & a soare throat For a sore throat aproved allthough it be a quincey A poulltes to Lay a swelling in ye face or neck or a quincey For a Thrush in a Childs mouth A spechall remidy to break ye bag of an Imposthum & so to spit it out by peice meale wth out hurting ye partie Mr Samuell Bradfords Balsam for a Cough & Consumption Mr Gilb#ls for ye same Another To cuer a cough,To help a consumption weakness or leanness For a Consumptive distemper For a cough To stop a cough or cuer it A Medicine For a scald head that hath a thick dry scab and noe Runinge scalde Another for ye same Another For ye same To cuer ye head ach For a gidyness in ye head. Mr Bradfords receipt,A Medicine For ye Leprosie Another or for a scabed face An approoved water or powder to further ye Labour of women A Medicine For one yt is torne in child bed An Exellent good Medecein for a women laboring with child Another for ye same Another for the same To further ye labor of wemen aproved A Medicine For one that is thick of heareing Another of the same approoved A medicine for ye hearinge when cold is ye cause of ye deafnesse or noise in ye head A fume for ye head or for ye hearinge wch hath recovered many To helpe ye hearinge,For ye noise in ye head aprooved For deafnesse and noise in ye head A Soveraigne medicine for ye paine or buringe in ye head wch hindereth ye hearinge Much To stop a cold ruhm yt troubles ye head To cuer one yt is deafe much approved A Medicine or Remedie For the Rupture or one that is Burst To make ye plaster A Receit aprooved for a Rupture,For ye falling sicknesse or convoltions. Do: Langhams way To make a suppository Another Another An aproved Medecine for the falling Siknes A Medicine for ye falling sicknes Another for the same,A Drink for a Cold or Coufe to cleare the bloud or against faintings. Lady Husseys A medciene for a cold Coufe or Consumption. Mrs #hunchens receipt A Surrup good for a coufe or ye king cough to remove theam & open ye stomach For a consumption Toe make ye Juice of locorish for a Cold Another Medicine For a Cold A medecen for a Cold To make iuce of Licoras another way Another way To stop a cough,For a Cold A Sirrop for a Cough For a Cough For any Cough A surup for a Consumption could. the Lady Alse cliftons receite Dr. Burges his Directions against ye plague Mr Dightons signes and sumetomes of ye plague with Directions For ye infected or not infected A Medicine for ye Plague,A Drinke that is good for ye longues or shorte breath For Shortnes of winde in a Sucking Child An Exelent medecine for ye Coufe & for stopage at ye brest & allso to open ye pipes & tow anoyd great store# of Curoption A very good drinke for to open ye pipes good for ye longes & spleane An Electuary for ye spleene A Medicine to cleanse and open spleen and remove obstructions An easie purge For a younge child Another easie purge A receite of pills very good for the head and stomacke experenced by maney. given by Mrs Robeson yt ws comended by Mr Marten ye apothycary for my Selfe to take R. F A pill for my Lady Fairfax agt fumes & wynds called Hyperitall & Hypor#ondriatall, afflutyu# H#n lungs, & causying faintying Pills for ye head & stomacke Jo: Faryes#,An Ointment to bee made in May and noe other time of ye yeare To make an Ointment for a Rume yt is fall yt runnes from ioynt to ioynt allso to kill a Tetter Toe make the oyntment called ye Lady oyntment that is good for hott Causes A Speciall ointment for any bruised place shakinge of ye hands or goute,A Receipt For a violent Burninge Feaver with a Loosenes An Exelent plaister to be appleyed to ye wristes to lay ye heate of a feaver Another for ye same that causeth rest & layes ye feaver A Sovereane drinke for a feavor to be given in ye hight of it An aproved drincke for one that is with Child & hath a burning feavor to Collet# Comfort A drinck for hec#iave feaver or any other feavor Toe kill a wild fire or inflamation To take away ye feaver A approoved glister Toe make a Restringent glister A glister for a weake bodey whose bowells are emptey it is streingthening as well as working ye paitent may take it being never soe weake,A tryed medicen for a Consumption A cullis for a consumption A Culles for a Consumption A approoved Medicine For a Consumption Another For ye same both to stay ye Cough and cleare ye Longues Toe make ye Jeley of hartshorne for one yt is on a Consumption An exellent Receipt for a Consumption. My Lady Cholmeleys Medicine An exellent Receipt for a Consumption or the Leannes of the lady by sicknes,A Receipt For ye evill called ye kings evill A perfect way to cure ye kings evill The Diet Drinke The Salve A medicine For ye toothach, Mr Dingly Another For ye same Mr Dingley Another For ye same Mr Dingley An aproved medeceine for ye toothake For ye toothake aproved To stay rume in ye teth An Excelent Receipt for ye tooth ach some say Infailable For ye tooth ach To cuer ye tooth ach Another,To make a very good purging drink. The lady Nutwys To make a pouder for the Green sicknesse wch folowes the foregoeing drink For purging A Diet drink of the Lady Hussey of Docter yarboroughs For a Consumption of Mr Bodingdikes or cough A diet drinke For the springe tyme An Exelent purging dyet drink against the scurve Jandis or dropse it opens the liver splene & longes it clears the bloud & is a generall good against any distemper & is aponted by Sr Thederan Myerane the is to be drunk spring & fall. this be Mrs Roson has exprence An aproved dyett drinck called the purging ale to be taken everey spring & fall From the first of Aprill to the end therof & from the midst of September to the midst of October,To make 3 gallons of Dyett drinke A Dyett Drinke to cleanse ye blood The Lady Norriges Dyett drinke Toe make a powder to unstopp ye liver Longues & milte The recepte of a drinke good against the fumes from the spleane & to open the obstrucitons. Mrs Robenson Recept A medecine for ye splen proved For ye spleene a generall purgation Do Lamons rare & never falling medicin for ye spleen,An Electurary for ye same Of the vertue of waters An Execlent surrup for ye spleen For ye spllen & Convouluisons,A Receipt for ye Rickets An aprooved Drinke for ye Ricketts The ointment For ye Ricketts aprooved A Nose for the disease commonly called ye Rickets A drinke for ye Rickets For children yt hath ye Ricketts aprooved For ye Ricits To streingthen Childrens limbs yt have ye Recets,To sweeten ye blood A drink to sweeten ye blood good for itchings or leperosie or kings evell by Do Lower To make a Sirrop of violetts or any other Floores To make surope of Reasons To make a Sirrop of violetts To make syrup of Violets,Dr Baggs his Sirrop of Marsh Mallows called Althea A reiceipt to make Surrup of Cloud gilliflowers To make Surrupe of violots or gilleflowers. The apotecarys way To make conserve of Red roses To make Surrupe of Voyolots,For to cuer ye stone For ye Colick,A Medicen for ye wind For ye Colick For ye Ston an excelent medicine For ye Ston Another for ye Ston For ye wind My Lady Ouslows Receipt for ye stone watter For ye ston & colick A powder for a woman that is licke to miscarey by reasons of weaknes or frites or spraines or fludes To presarve wemen from misCarigae Another for ye same. Thes tow are my Lord Chesterfillds A Stringthing Elixtorey to be taken in any Weakenes espechelley upon feare of miscarcying To Bring Throwes on a woman in travell when she is Spent & hath not streingth to bring forth a dead Child or after birth if her throwes have left her it will cause throwes to Come againe,To prevent Abortion an Aproved Plaister Called ye Countes of Moulgraves A Receipt to prevent miscarrying sent to my Sister Cartwright by her neece ye Duchesse of Buckingham This note under written send to ye Apothecery To stop ye Flowers in wemen wn they flood or have ym Immoderatly,An Exellent Receipt to stay ye flowers in women if they come immodrately For ye staying off ye flowers when they immodarat often aproved For ye same For thos wch are apt to miscarry To Bring downe them when they stope in young maides Another for the same,For ye palsie An oyntmt for any old ach or numness in ye Joynts An Excelent remedy for ye palsie in ye head For ye Dead Palsy aprooved or any benumd nesse in the ioynts A Receit for ye Dead Palsie A Receit for ye Palsy For ye Palsy in ye longue Another for ye same,How to make a decocktion of Gyiacum To cleans ye blood To streingthen ye liver & hart Walnutt water thus distilled is good against the Plague or any infectious diseases by sicknes The Walnutt water called the water of life for the vertues therof The vertues of the simple Walnutt water An Excellent Cordiall water For a surfet,The Lady Allens water, good for Surfetts of meate small Pox Feavers, or measels To make a good water. Dr Thovalds Receiptes To make strawbery water To make yt Surfit watter of Red poppyes To make stomack water Lady Canarvans way Cordialls watters To make Chereys watter A Receipt to make Balme watter A Cordiall watter to be taken with Surope of Clove Gilleflowers by any one that is Sick or faint To make Saforon watter To make Cinamond watter,An Exelent aqua mirable An Exelent surfett watter A Receipt of treacle watter Docter Denhams #quitesons for surfets & feavers A watter to cause one to sweat To make popey watter A cordiall watter of Chereyes good for fainting fits Mr Gascons powder,To make The Cordiall powder A Recipt to make Spiritt of Clougille flowers A Cordiall Electuarye. La: Darces A good Surfet watter To make ye same called milk watter To make ye plague watter An Excelent receipt for ye plague wch did help 600 in york & in one house wher 8 were Infected 2 of ym drunk of it & lived ye other would not & dyed To make a good surfet watter of popeys Lady winson way,To make a good surfet watter of popeys Mrs Purtrys The Merquess of Dorchesters Cordiall water used by the Faimly of ye Pierponts,Receipts for Ciurgerey, A Soveran oyle for to drope in to wounds hurt with vermin weapons or pines pricked Mr Moullers fomentation for an extream ach or Lamnes A green oyle for a bruse An Exelent receipte for a bruse all though it be never soe bad in any parte of ye limes To Make ye Paraceltion plaster,The Virtues of this plaster followeth An exelent salve to cuer any wound or sore For a bruse A Receipt for a violent ache that is come with a heate & Could A watter to Cleanse an owld sore of ye dead flesh & to hele ye sore A wound drinke A drinke to cuer an old soare A Sercloth for a spraine A plaster for ye paine in ye Stomake,The oyle of Charity good for all maner of Swellings & for nuw & old sors by way of Plasters use it Cold as it is A Curious Ballsom for any soare by yt Exelent Venetion Signior Lucattella A Salve for a green cutt or wound To draw out a Thorne A Soveran Medeceine for one biten with a Made doge or Stung with an adder To helpe a bruse or spraine For an ach or paine in ye bake or bones A Receipt for any aches or Burnings or Scalldings A purge for ye Kings evill Sr Kenelin Digbies diet Ale for a Fistula or dropsy A Diet drink for kings evill or any cold rewnes For the pallat of the Mouth For to knit a rib yt is broken or any inward bruse Good for a Bruse,To make oyle of Mace For ye kings Evell The yalow salve good to draw a Thorn out of ye flesh or any festered sore yt needeth to be drawn. Lady Husseys yalow salve To make oyle of Almonds or Nutts To make oyle Almonds Wallnutts & small nutts Oyle of Roses To make ye oyle of snails,To take ye fire out of a scald or burne To tak fire out of a burn To cuer a burn Spedly or Scald Another To make a pultice to get fire out of a Scald or burn To goud ye fire out of a burn Another How to prepare worms for powder An openinge possett drinke to take with ye powder The purge to be taken once a weeke all ye while yw take ye powder followinge,A Receit to make Woorm wood Cakes,For pluerese when a man is past blud leting,A Dyett drinke for faintnesse or ye scurry An aproved good dyet dinke to be taken in ye Springe or fall An Excelent dyet drink to be given wth Scury grasse agt ye dropsey & Scurye,A Glister for ye Gout The dyett drinke for ye Goutt A Pultise for ye Goutt An excellent plaister for ye goutt or aches strains or weaknesses in ye bones or Synews beinge applyd to ye place greived and for ye weakenesse and paine in ye backe for fitts of ye mother and to suppresse melancholly vapors yt fly from ye Spleen to ye head and to Strengthen a weake stomach This is most effectuall applyd to ye stomach lett itt ly till itt will sticke no longer it will allso breake a plague sore. Mrs French Barringtons dryed plaister The lady Greys drinke for ye gout,An aproved remedy for a scald head. my Lady Hasseys often approved To make haire grow wch Comes of wth any accedeint of Sicknesse or sore head Mrs Trusells receipt who has sold ye water for 50 Shillings a pint & 30 ye lowest rate For a scald head or to cuer any scabes For a scurfe in ye head yt whit,For the Stone an Electury For ye Stone For ye Stone in ye Kidneys For ye Stone very good and to open obstructions For ye Stone For ye Collicke stone or Strangory For ye Collicke For ye Stone & colike For ye stone,A Medicine For any in ward bleedinge. My Lady Temples,Good agt a Loosnes or griping A Glister for ye Gripes and loosenes. prescribed by Dr Trisse A Clister for the Gripes without loosenes For all sorts of paines or Impedims in ye Head The flower of Salves For a numbd palsey For His Phrensy & Tissick For grips & aproved remedy yt cureth Speedyly For all maner of Sores in the Eyes a precious water Mrs Loes Receipt of Flos unguentorid: 1699 The vertues For gripes or loosness To stop ye Coming down of ye tearms by a midwife To bring ym down in young maids,A drinke for thos yt have sore eyes or for clearing ye blood For Decay of sight For any Inflamations or Ruhme in ye eyes For eyes yt are gumod up For ye Eyes For sore eyes wn blood shot or sweled An Electuary for a loosness To stop a loosness or Flux it has cured thos yt have gone 30 tymes a day Lady Husseys Another For gripes or a loosnesse For gripes or a loosnesse For a loosnesse For a loosnese,For Fransies or inflamations of ye braine,A powder to take away a wean Another for ye Same For ye dropsie To cause Urine where there is a Totall obstruction thereof or for a dropse very good Do Willis Receipt for a dropsey To cuer a dropsie or leges yt are swell'd A drinke they may take at ther meales & all tymes for a dropsie A Receipt for ye dropsie,Take this Cawdall A Receit for of a Gravy to Strenghten ye stomack yt cannot keepe their meat. The lady Husseys A Receit to Strengthen one in great weakenesse. Mrs Chalenot,For a Consumption of ye longes For a Cough. Dr Fryar Apr. 1669 To make plain coole dyet'atecall Chocelet Sr James Lehgrams way An electuary for ye head to Comfort ye memory clear ye sight strengthen ye Stomack To streinghten ye memory,To cuer one yt piteth thir bed For wind in ye Stomach An Electuary for melancholly wind in ye Stomach y fumes to ye head A Purge. Mrs Robensons purge For Cold Fleame in ye Stomach For wind in ye Stomach,A Gentle purge,Balsam cephalic Emplaster Arthritic,A Streingthing Pill for ye head & nerves from Sr James Langham A pill for ye head To make pills for ye Stomach A pill comended for mee R.F. by Docter Catlin, 1653 wn I was very ill called Vatican pilles A pill given to me by Docter Bathurst for rumes A Catholicall purging powder. Do Langhams A pill good for ye dropsey,The Receipt of ye Tar Pill, Good agt. a Cough- of ye Countese of Westmerlands,For ye pyles Another for ye same,A Soverain Remedy for a sore breast or any other Swelling The Yellow Salve of my Lady Husseys 29 Sep: 1684 A medecin for a strain to be used at ye first geting it or after To make a Salve Called Flos unguentium,An Excelent Poultice for a sore breast Another Pultice to break a sore breast For a milk sore in ye breast Snail water Good against a Consumption or hective feavor. Comended by my La St Johns,An approved Salve to heal & draw any Impostim fistilow or sweling in any part of ye body To kill an Itching in wounds wn healing or Scabs A very drawing Poultice to make a hard swellinge breake To make a pultice for a sore breast Another pultice good to break sore breasts or boyls,A good Poultice for any swelling A good Pultice yt cuers sore breast or hard swelling making ym to breake & cuers wth out salves To make a watter yt cuers wounds & sores wthout plaisters A pultice to break hard swellings A very Soverian Oyntment for any Ach or payne Swelling or bruise or sore breast & likewise to make a Salve of it for any sore To make ye Oyntment A Receipt of Alderman Langhams wife of a sere cloth allmost for any thinge as aches swellings Sprains Soares, Bruses, cuts, To draw and heale if not an hot humour,Isues a good plaster To cuer ye Itch Another Another Do Lowers dyet drinke to clear ye blood for ye Leporsie & Itching distempers For ye Itch To make a watter to kill a teter To make a Cooling oyntmt To cuer ye Itch,To cuer a tetter A Receipt of an exelent Ballsom good for all sores inward or outward of Sr Richard Knapen The La: Nu##eys To make oyl of Swallows Against a bruse To make oyl of Swalows good for ye hearing,A Receit given under hand and Seale by a Southerne phisition to the Sherriff of york as a Speciall gratification for Some favours done to him by the Sherriff For one yt hath ye Scury in his mouth,A Receipt of a rare Convoultion water Mrs Garderen at Steford A Convoulsion watter approved & made by Maior Generall Lambert To cuer convoulsions,For ye bitting of a mad dog To draw out ye venome of one bit wth a mad dog. Mr Boyles way Good for any that are bitt wth Serpints,To stay vomiting To Strengthen ye Stomach For ye Colick Do: Shorts way to Cuer ye Colick For ye Colick Dr Lowers Collick watter,To cuer a scrath or sore yt comes any other way An approved medecein for ye gout,A very rare Receipt of Mis Huttons wch cuered her of a cancer in her breast & for wch 3 score pound had been given for ye receipt An Espechell medicin for Fitts of ye Moer to put it clean away To cuer ye fits of ye Mother To cuer ye fits of ye Mother,To prevent miscarrying Commended by ye Dutches of devenshir A receipt for Childblanes aproved A Salve to cuer a green wound a Scald & Chilblanes Take sope & doe your fingers wth it & this cuers them let it be done over night To prevent a blow turning blake To cuer childblans if not broke,For ye Jandise Another for ye Jaundice Another for ye Jaundice A Plaster for a Ru#nseg,For ye Boyles For Inflamations & swellings A good purg for thos yt have had boyles or kings Evell sores. Mr Edgleys purge To break a boyle or Plague sore To break A Fellon or Anancom Good for ye pilles Another for ye pilles Another for ye pilles A rare Receipt for ye Scurvy Another for ye same Do: Rants rare watter for ye Scurve or dropsie Do Fryen Receipt for ye Scurvie For ye Scurvie or mangie For ye Scurvi For ye scurvi Mrs Dones Receipt,For spiting of blood For spiting by ruhme For wattering Eyes For eyes yt have a speke in them it takes it away in a litle tyme To draw a ruhme from ye eyes For sore eyes or red eyes wth ruhme For eyes yt have skins or mists before ym used & Commended by do: Thurberwck an Oculist An Aproved medicien for ye eyes,For ye kings evell A dyet drinke to purge ye blood to cuer y kings eveill ye Lady Sr Barbais recipt Good agt Poyson To cuer a scald or burn A whit oyntment Excelent for scalds or burns; To stop flooding in Childbed to cuer the green Sicknesse Good for bruses & ackes Another Another To skin a place new brused Another to skin a sore Good for a Inward bruse To skine a new sore For a bite of a viper or adder; An Excelent powder to make one sweat & take away pains & good for Agues, & ye Bloody flux A snaile watter good for a Consumption; For sore niples To kill Rats.

msItem.author: Brumwich, Anne
msItem.title: Brumwich, Anne (& others)
msItem.filiation: Musgrave's [i]Obituary[/i] has an entry of the death of Anne Bromwich in 1628, the daughter of Edward Trotman, a lawyer of Gloucester, who may be the original compiler of this MS. It was certainly owned, and largely added to by Rhoda Hussey, daughter of Thomas Chapman of London. Her second husband was Baron Ferdinando Fairfax of Cameron [1584-1648], whom she married in 1646, and by whom she had a daughter, Ursula. Her initials are stamped on the covers (R.H.) and her signature as 'Rhoda Hussey' and 'Rhoda Fairfax' are found on the first fly-leaf, together with that of her daughter Ursula Fairfax [1647-1702], dated 30 March 1665. She died in 1686.
Hashmarks (#) in the summar are used to indicate where the indexers were unable to read the original text.

objectDesc.supportDesc: 1 volume2 ll.+288 pp.+4 bl. ll.+11 ll. folio. 301/2 x 19 cm. Contemporary gilt-stamped calf binding, worn: with centre ornament, and lettered 'R.H.' Both clasps wanting. The second fly-leaf is mostly torn away. Pp. 77/8, 107/8 and 212/13 are missing, and a leaf has been cut from among the four blank leaves towards the end of the volume.

binding.p: 2 ll.+288 pp.+4 bl. ll.+11 ll. folio. 301/2 x 19 cm. Contemporary gilt-stamped calf binding, worn: with centre ornament, and lettered 'R.H.' Both clasps wanting. The second fly-leaf is mostly torn away. Pp. 77/8, 107/8 and 212/13 are missing, and a leaf has been cut from among the four blank leaves towards the end of the volume.

bibl.title: Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library
bibl.author: S. A. J. Moorat
bibl.publisher: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine

custodialHist.custEvent: Database description transcribed from S.A.J. Moorat, [i]Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library[/i] (London: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine, 1962-1973).

additional.surrogates: In addition, a microfilm copy is held as AMS/MF/232.

listBibl.bibl: Published (in microfilm form) in [i]Women in Medicine: Remedy Books, 1533 - 1865[/i], ed. Sara Pennell (Woodbridge, Connecticut: Primary Source Microfilm, 2004).

revisionDesc.change: Transformed Calm xml export to TEI xml file using a transformation scenario
Manual edits following automated Calm-TEI transformation
